title: Scope and rate-limit backfills
description: Run backfills over explicit date ranges with bounded concurrency so they reproduce history without overwhelming shared resources.
body: Use when reprocessing a historical date range after a fix or new model. Do not kick off an unbounded backfill across all dates at full parallelism — it can saturate the warehouse and starve live runs. Actions: confirm tasks are idempotent per date, bound backfill concurrency and pool slots, run an explicit date range, and reconcile a sample of backfilled output against expectations. Validation signals include backfilled partitions matching the original scheduled output and live runs unaffected by backfill load. Risk boundary: an uncontrolled backfill can exhaust shared capacity and double-write; if tasks are not idempotent or capacity is tight, deny the bulk backfill and escalate to stage it.
